name: Albendin from Baena — circular via the Greenway of Oil
name_original: Albendín desde Baena — circular por la Vía Verde del Aceite
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Baena are primarily shaped by the region's expansive olive groves and its connection to the Vía Verde del Aceite. This greenway, built on an old railway line, features historic metallic bridges and tunnels, traversing varied landscapes including parts of the Sierra Subbéticas. The area offers a network of routes that combine agricultural scenery with natural features. Best touring cycling routes around Baena The most popular touring cycling route is Albendin from Baena — circular via the Greenway of Oil, a 34.0 miles (54.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows a section of the Vía Verde del Aceite. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Zuheros Castle and Village – Doña Mencía Station loop from Baena, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.8 km) path. This route offers views of a historic castle and village, winding through the local landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Guadajoz River Viaduct – Víboras River Viaduct loop from Baena, a 52.0 miles (83.7 km) trail leading through areas featuring old railway viaducts, often completed in about 5 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Baena is defined by extensive olive groves, the Vía Verde del Aceite, and the rolling terrain of the Sierra Subbéticas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Baena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 26 reviews. More than 310 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Baena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of touring cycling routes can I expect around Baena?
A: Baena offers an extensive network of touring cycling routes, primarily characterized by vast olive groves and its connection to the renowned Vía Verde del Aceite. These routes follow old railway lines, featuring historic metallic bridges and tunnels, and traverse diverse landscapes including parts of the Sierra Subbéticas. You'll find options ranging from moderate to difficult, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Baena?
A: While many routes in Baena are rated moderate to difficult, the Vía Verde del Aceite itself is generally well-suited for families due to its gentle gradients as a former railway line. Look for sections that are shorter and flatter for a more relaxed family experience. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle and Village – Doña Mencía Station loop from Baena&lt;/a&gt; is rated moderate and offers scenic views.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Baena?
A: The optimal times for touring cycling in Baena are during &lt;strong&gt;spring&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;autumn&lt;/strong&gt; when the weather is most pleasant. The months of January and December are also noted as popular times, likely due to milder winter conditions compared to other regions, making it suitable for year-round cycling.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available from Baena?
A: Yes, Baena features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025893453/albendin-from-baena-circular-via-the-greenway-of-oil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albendin from Baena — circular via the Greenway of Oil&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 54.7 km trail. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle and Village – Doña Mencía Station loop from Baena&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of a historic castle and village.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Baena offer views of historical and natural attractions. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle through areas featuring old railway viaducts, such as on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33872909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadajoz River Viaduct – Víboras River Viaduct loop from Baena&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4050527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Río&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bat Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in the Baena area?
A: The Baena area boasts an extensive network of touring cycling routes, with over 240 different paths available for exploration. This includes a variety of lengths and elevation gains, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Baena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Baena are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from over 26 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique experience of riding through expansive olive groves and along the historic Vía Verde del Aceite, with its distinctive bridges and tunnels.

Q: Are there any routes that feature tunnels or old railway structures?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde del Aceite, which is central to touring cycling in Baena, follows an old railway line and is known for its historic metallic bridges and tunnels. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31415113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Steam locomotive - Mikado – Plantío Tunnel loop from Baena&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight these unique features, including the Plantío Tunnel.

Q: Can I find routes that offer challenging climbs or longer distances?
A: Yes, Baena offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. Many of the routes are rated 'difficult,' with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33872909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadajoz River Viaduct – Víboras River Viaduct loop from Baena&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 83.7 km trail with substantial climbing, often completed in about 5 hours 23 minutes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments to see while cycling?
A: Yes, the region around Baena features natural attractions that can be incorporated into your cycling adventures. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3153745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreras de la Fuenseca&lt;/a&gt;, a waterfall, or enjoy views over the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4751189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Conde&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument.

Q: What is the Vía Verde del Aceite and how does it relate to Baena?
A: The Vía Verde del Aceite (Greenway of Olive Oil) is one of Spain's longest greenways, stretching over 100 kilometers, and Baena is a key point along its path. A 7.9-kilometer branch specifically connects Baena to this popular route. It's an old railway line converted into a cycling path, offering a unique journey through olive groves, historic bridges, and tunnels, and is ideal for cycle tourism.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through villages or offer cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, some routes provide opportunities to explore local villages and cultural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle and Village – Doña Mencía Station loop from Baena&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers views of a historic castle and village, allowing cyclists to experience the local culture and scenery.
tips: Tempting stop on the route if you are doing the greenway: the old Luque station now houses a bar that invites you to stop and recharge your batteries.

In general, all the viaducts of the Greenway of the Oil were built in France in the city of Creil by Daydé and Pillé.
